sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel movement data documented for SNES port

special_mechanics.name: Aircraft Control
special_mechanics.description: Player controls experimental fighter aircraft (A8M5/A8M6) with joystick. First-person perspective with camera shift to third-person behind aircraft when locked onto by enemy missiles for evasive maneuvers.

special_mechanics.name: Dual Weapon System
special_mechanics.description: Aircraft equipped with cannon (continuous fire) and missiles (targeted weapons). Player can lock onto targets by moving crosshair and launching missiles.

special_mechanics.name: Motion Simulator Cabinet Physics
special_mechanics.description: R360 arcade cabinet uses gyroscope-based two-axis rotation mounted on stationary base. Cabinet responds dynamically to pilot actions, affecting perceived flight dynamics and movement feel.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Qualitative/empirical data only. G-LOC is a combat flight simulator/shmup hybrid, not a character platformer. Movement parameters (walk speed, jump physics, gravity) are not applicable to aircraft-based gameplay. SNES port documentation lacks detailed physics specifications. Game emphasizes arcade feel via motion cabinet feedback rather than precise movement metrics.
sources_used: Wikipedia - G-LOC: Air Battle (gameplay section, release/port information)
